label: Traditional / ancient
summary: The dominant ancient itinerary: most episodes cluster around Sicily and southern Italy.
key_ids: Cyclops in SE Sicily; Aeolus at Lipari; Laestrygonians in Sicily; Scylla/Charybdis at the Strait of Messina; Lotus-Eaters at Djerba.

label: Berard
summary: The most detailed modern western-Med reconstruction; adopted as standard in the 1959 Atlas of the Classical World.
key_ids: Djerba; Posillipo (Naples); Stromboli; N. Sardinia; Monte Circeo; Cumae; Messina; Calypso near Gibraltar.

label: Bradford
summary: A Royal Navy veteran's refinement of the western-Med route, sailed in person.
key_ids: Cyclops near Marsala/Favignana ('Goat Island'); Aeolus at Ustica; Laestrygonians at Bonifacio; Sirens at Li Galli; Calypso on Malta.

label: Severin
summary: Sailed a replica Bronze Age galley Troy-to-Ithaca in a single 3-4 month season; landfalls sit at natural turning points near Greece.
key_ids: Laestrygonians at Mezapos (Mani); Hades at the Acheron/Necromanteion; Circe at Paxos; Aeolus at Gramvousa; Scheria = Corfu.

label: Atlantic / fringe
summary: Places the wanderings in a fairyland out in the Atlantic; refuted by Polybius using the 9-day sail from Cape Malea.
key_ids: Underworld far out in the Atlantic; Ogygia and Scheria 'imagined in fantasy' west of Gibraltar / Britain.
